topic: Skyroot Aerospace reaches orbit with Vikram-1 Rocket
headline: SKYROOT AEROSPACE BECOMES THIRD COUNTRY WITH PRIVATE ORBITAL LAUNCH CAPABILITY WITH SUCCESSFUL VIKRAM-1 ROCKET LAUNCH
summary: Skyroot Aerospace has launched India's first private orbital class rocket, Vikram-1, into orbit. The launch makes India the third country with a private orbital launch capability. Vikram-1 is a four-stage small-lift launch vehicle that successfully reached orbit on July 18, 2026.

sections.paragraph: The history of India's space industry is marked by significant developments, particularly in recent years. The industry, driven by the Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO), has grown to include over 500 private suppliers and various bodies of the Department of Space. Since the sector was opened to the private sector in 2020, numerous private space companies have emerged, with over 400 private space enterprises as of 2024, up from 54 in 2020. The government has also taken steps to support the sector, including the announcement of a $119 million venture capital fund for space startups in 2024. The private sector's role in the space industry has been expanding, with companies like Bellatrix Aerospace setting up offices and manufacturing facilities in the US, and the number of space startups in India increasing, with over 300 startups involved in various stages of developing launch vehicles and satellites as of mid-2025.

sources.title: ISRO's role in India's private space sector boom | Explained

sections.paragraph: The technology angle of the Tata Tiago reveals that the electric hatchback will feature Multi-Mode Regenerative braking, cruise control, and Sports Mode. This technology allows users to recharge batteries while driving, with the highest regen level aiding in single-pedal driving. The Multi-Mode Regenerative braking was first introduced in the Nexon EV Max and then on the Nexon EV Prime, and it is expected to be available in other EVs in Tata's portfolio, including the Tigor EV. The sources do not provide information on how this technology will be implemented in the Tiago, but it is mentioned that the Tiago EV will launch on September 28 and is said to be the brand's most economical EV. In contrast, other sources discuss unrelated topics, such as a resort's tennis activities, and do not provide relevant information on the technology angle of the Tata Tiago.

sources.title: Tata Tiago with 3 new features teased

sections.paragraph: Skyroot Aerospace, an Indian private aerospace manufacturer, has made significant strides in the business of launch services, becoming the first private company in India to launch a suborbital rocket, Vikram-S, and later India's first private orbital class rocket, Vikram-1. This achievement makes India the third country, after the U.S. and China, with a private orbital launch capability. The company aims to develop commercially competitive launch vehicles for the global small-satellite market and has signed agreements with other companies, such as Nibe Space and Axiom Space, to increase access to Low Earth Orbit (LEO) and launch constellations of satellites. Skyroot Aerospace plans to produce one orbital rocket per month, indicating a potential for significant business growth in the private launch services sector. The company's partnerships and technological advancements, including the development of the Vikram-1 and Vikram-2 launch vehicles, demonstrate its commitment to expanding its business in the space industry.

A new study on India's space economy emphasizes the need for evidence-based policies and supportive regulations to expand the space sector. The study highlights significant growth and transformation in the Indian space sector due to increasing private-sector involvement. Researchers have identified gaps in existing literature on the space economy, underscoring the need for updated and comprehensive analyses to guide future policies. This, in turn, is expected to strengthen India's status in the global space arena. The call for supportive regulations and evidence-based policies suggests a desire to create an enabling environment for private players, public-private partnerships, and innovation in the space sector. Overall, the study aims to inform policies that can facilitate the continued growth of India's space economy.

sources.title: Expanding space economy: new study calls for evidence-based policies, supportive regulations - The Hindu
